reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own puzzle is correct (88 - 51 = 37), and based on all previous games the others’ puzzles are also simple arithmetic, so the chance that B, C, or D made a mistake is very small. Verifying would cost coins and still risks a random death if everyone verifies or passes, as in Game 1. By volunteering immediately using each player’s own capsule set (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D), I avoid the random-elimination scenario and very likely secure a correct password without spending any coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know Q2-B is correct because it’s my own puzzle (50 ÷ 10 = 5). All four questions are straightforward arithmetic, so the others are very likely correct as in previous games. Verifying would cost coins and still leave a risk of random death if no one volunteers. Volunteering now with the only available capsules maximizes my chance to escape with all 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: The puzzles have always been straightforward arithmetic and, across previous games, no capsule answer has ever been wrong. I’m very confident in my own solution to Q3 and have no specific reason to doubt the others. Verifying would cost a coin and still leave the risk of random death if no one volunteers, and passing also risks a random elimination I can’t control. By volunteering immediately with the single available capsule for each question, I avoid the random-death risk, keep all 4 coins if (as is very likely) the password is correct, and end the game efficiently.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
